# vcfkit — Full Documentation

> Fast, single-binary VCF toolkit — normalize, liftover, filter — validated against bcftools. No htslib, no Python, no C dependencies.

vcfkit is a Rust CLI for bioinformaticians. It does three operations every VCF pipeline needs, rewritten for speed and correctness. 4× faster than bcftools on normalize and filter hot paths, validated nightly on 1000 Genomes chr22 data. The same core compiles to WebAssembly and runs in the browser at vcfkit.dev.

Not for clinical use. Research and pipeline tooling only.

---

## Install

### Homebrew (macOS / Linux)
```
brew install vcfkit
```

### Pre-built binaries

macOS Apple Silicon:
```
curl -L https://github.com/robertlangdonn/vcfkit/releases/latest/download/vcfkit-aarch64-apple-darwin.tar.gz | tar xz
sudo mv vcfkit /usr/local/bin/
```

macOS Intel:
```
curl -L https://github.com/robertlangdonn/vcfkit/releases/latest/download/vcfkit-x86_64-apple-darwin.tar.gz | tar xz
sudo mv vcfkit /usr/local/bin/
```

Linux x86_64:
```
curl -L https://github.com/robertlangdonn/vcfkit/releases/latest/download/vcfkit-x86_64-unknown-linux-musl.tar.gz | tar xz
sudo mv vcfkit /usr/local/bin/
```

Linux ARM64:
```
curl -L https://github.com/robertlangdonn/vcfkit/releases/latest/download/vcfkit-aarch64-unknown-linux-musl.tar.gz | tar xz
sudo mv vcfkit /usr/local/bin/
```

Windows: download vcfkit-x86_64-pc-windows-msvc.zip from GitHub Releases and add to PATH.

Install script (macOS/Linux):
```
curl -fsSL https://vcfkit.dev/install.sh | sh
```

### Cargo
```
cargo install vcfkit-cli
```
Requires Rust 1.75+.

### Shell completions
```
vcfkit completions bash >> ~/.bashrc
vcfkit completions zsh >> ~/.zshrc
vcfkit completions fish > ~/.config/fish/completions/vcfkit.fish
```

---

## normalize

Left-align indels, split multi-allelic sites into biallelic records, and optionally validate REF alleles against a reference FASTA.

### Synopsis
```
vcfkit normalize [OPTIONS] --reference <FASTA> [INPUT]
```

### Options
- `-f, --reference <FASTA>` — Reference genome FASTA (required)
- `-o, --output <FILE>` — Output file (default: stdout)
- `--no-split` — Keep multi-allelic sites (don't split)
- `--no-left-align` — Skip left-alignment of indels
- `--check-ref <MODE>` — REF mismatch handling: ignore / warn (default) / error
- `--fast` — Fast path for biallelic SNPs/MNPs (~4× faster)
- `-q, --quiet` — Suppress progress bar

### Examples
```
# Standard: left-align + split multi-allelic
vcfkit normalize -f hg38.fa input.vcf > normalized.vcf

# Fast path
vcfkit normalize --fast -f hg38.fa input.vcf > normalized.vcf

# Keep multi-allelic sites
vcfkit normalize -f hg38.fa --no-split input.vcf

# Strict REF validation
vcfkit normalize -f hg38.fa --check-ref error input.vcf
```

### bcftools equivalence
- `vcfkit normalize -f ref.fa` ≈ `bcftools norm -f ref.fa -m-any -c w`
- `vcfkit normalize -f ref.fa --no-split` ≈ `bcftools norm -f ref.fa -c w`

### Known differences
Multi-allelic indels are passed through unchanged (not left-aligned). Biallelic indel left-alignment is fully implemented. Joint multi-allelic left-alignment is planned for v0.2.

---

## liftover

Convert variant coordinates between genome builds using UCSC chain files.

### Synopsis
```
vcfkit liftover [OPTIONS] --source <FASTA> --target <FASTA> --chain <CHAIN> [INPUT]
```

### Options
- `-s, --source <FASTA>` — Source genome FASTA
- `-t, --target <FASTA>` — Target genome FASTA
- `-c, --chain <CHAIN>` — UCSC chain file (.chain or .chain.gz)
- `-o, --output <FILE>` — Output file (default: stdout)
- `-r, --reject <FILE>` — Write unmapped records here
- `--write-src-coords` — Add INFO/SRC_CONTIG and INFO/SRC_POS to output
- `--allow-contig-mismatch` — Suppress contig-name mismatch error (b37 vs UCSC naming)

### Examples
```
# hg19 → hg38
vcfkit liftover -s hg19.fa -t hg38.fa -c hg19ToHg38.over.chain.gz input.vcf -o output.vcf

# Keep rejected records
vcfkit liftover -s hg19.fa -t hg38.fa -c hg19ToHg38.over.chain.gz -r rejects.vcf input.vcf

# b37 VCF with UCSC chain (suppress contig error)
vcfkit liftover --allow-contig-mismatch -s b37.fa -t hg38.fa -c hg19ToHg38.over.chain.gz input.vcf
```

### Chain file download
```
# hg19 → hg38
wget https://hgdownload.soe.ucsc.edu/goldenPath/hg19/liftOver/hg19ToHg38.over.chain.gz

# hg38 → hg19
wget https://hgdownload.soe.ucsc.edu/goldenPath/hg38/liftOver/hg38ToHg19.over.chain.gz

# hg38 → T2T-CHM13v2.0
wget https://hgdownload.soe.ucsc.edu/goldenPath/hg38/liftOver/hg38ToHs1.over.chain.gz
```

---

## filter

Keep variants matching an expression over VCF fields.

### Synopsis
```
vcfkit filter [OPTIONS] --expression <EXPR> [INPUT]
```

### Options
- `-e, --expression <EXPR>` — Filter expression (mutually exclusive with --ask)
- `-a, --ask <QUERY>` — Natural-language query, translated via Anthropic API
- `--yes` — Skip confirmation when using --ask
- `--accept-low-confidence` — Proceed when translation confidence is below 50%
- `-o, --output <FILE>` — Output file (default: stdout)
- `-v, --invert` — Keep records that do NOT match
- `-q, --quiet` — Suppress progress bar

### Examples
```
# Rare variants
vcfkit filter -e "INFO/AF < 0.01" input.vcf

# High quality PASS variants
vcfkit filter -e "QUAL > 30 && FILTER == 'PASS'" input.vcf

# Region filter
vcfkit filter -e "CHROM == 'chr17' && POS >= 43044295 && POS <= 43125483" input.vcf

# Substring match
vcfkit filter -e "INFO/CSQ ~ 'missense'" input.vcf

# Inverted filter
vcfkit filter -e "FILTER == 'PASS'" --invert input.vcf

# From stdin
bcftools view input.bcf | vcfkit filter -e "INFO/DP > 10"
```

### Expression language

Fields:
- `INFO/<key>` — per INFO field type (Float, Integer, String)
- `FORMAT/<key>` — first sample only
- `CHROM` — string, e.g. 'chr1'
- `POS` — integer, 1-based
- `QUAL` — float; missing (.) evaluates to false
- `FILTER` — string, e.g. 'PASS'

Operators: `<`, `<=`, `>`, `>=`, `==`, `!=`, `&&`, `||`, `!`, `~` (substring), `!~`

String literals use single quotes: `'PASS'`, `'chr1'`

Multi-allelic INFO fields with Number=A use any-element semantics: `INFO/AF < 0.01` matches if any ALT allele has AF < 0.01.

### Natural-language filter (--ask)

Requires `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`. Translates plain English to a deterministic filter expression using Claude. You review the expression before it runs.

```
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...

# Interactive (shows expression for review)
vcfkit filter --ask "rare PASS variants on chromosome 17" input.vcf

# Non-interactive
vcfkit filter -a "rare PASS variants" --yes input.vcf
```

The LLM sees: your query + the VCF header schema (INFO/FORMAT field names, types, descriptions).
Variant data (CHROM, POS, REF, ALT, genotypes) never leaves your machine.

When confidence < 50%, --yes is blocked. Add --accept-low-confidence to override.
Requires an input file path; stdin is not supported.
Not available in the browser demo.

---

## Performance

Measured on 1000 Genomes chr22 (1,103,547 variants), macOS aarch64, bcftools 1.23.1:

| Operation                     | vcfkit    | bcftools  | Speedup  |
|-------------------------------|-----------|-----------|----------|
| filter -e 'INFO/AF < 0.01'    | 422 ms    | 1,695 ms  | 4.0×     |
| normalize --fast --no-split   | 682 ms    | 2,820 ms  | 4.1×     |
| normalize (standard path)     | 6,481 ms  | 2,820 ms  | 0.43×    |
| liftover                      | 6,713 ms  | —         | ~164K rec/s |

The fast path applies to biallelic SNPs/MNPs. Standard normalize is slower than bcftools because it uses a pure-Rust parser (noodles) rather than htslib's C implementation. The fast path is representative for typical variant call VCFs.

---

## Known differences from bcftools

1. Multi-allelic indel left-alignment: passed through unchanged in v0.1.x. Fix planned for v0.2.
2. All other operations match bcftools output in differential tests on 1000 Genomes chr22.

---

## Privacy

- CLI operations: no network access, no data sent anywhere
- Telemetry: opt-in only, configured at ~/.config/vcfkit/config.toml, never includes variant data
- --ask: sends your query text + VCF header schema to Anthropic API. Variant data (CHROM, POS, REF, ALT, genotypes) never sent.
- Browser demo: all processing in WebAssembly, nothing uploaded

---

## Source

https://github.com/robertlangdonn/vcfkit
